name: St. Gallen (Switzerland)
description: A square green field charged with an upright white fasces, a bundle of rods bound around an axe.
history: An original 1803 design by David von Gonzenbach marking the canton's creation from eight territories. The blade was a halberd until changed to an axe in 1843; a black Swiss cross was briefly placed on the blade (1930s-1951) to avoid association with Italian Fascism.

features.role: Roman fasces (a bundle of rods bound around an axe), upright
features.symbolism: the union of eight territories into one canton in 1803
